appliedUpdates.description: Documented that shadcn overlay primitives (Dialog, Sheet, Popover) should stay on the same BETTER near-black, white-foreground, restrained-border, 8px-radius contract as the main card surfaces.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the frontend-experience-worker baseline-validation and test-first procedure for release/polish follow-up work: if baseline validation exposes a clearly in-scope regression, workers may repair it using either a focused new failing test or an already-failing scoped regression test before full revalidation, without having to mark the run as a procedure deviation solely for that reason.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviewed features reported successful scoped regression repair work but had to mark `followedProcedure=false` because the current skill wording is stricter than the workflow that actually produced the fix. `polish-visual-audit-and-regressions` used a focused failing contract test plus full reruns after baseline validation exposed the issue, and `polish-push-and-build-verification` relied on the already-failing `card-theme-contract` regression to repair the same release-blocking theme contract.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align the final-polish validation contract with feature-level release requirements by either adding the README-refresh expectation to the milestone contract or explicitly marking it as non-validator scope.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The reviewed release feature `polish-push-and-build-verification` requires `Update README with new content sections` in mission `features.json`, but the final-polish contract section only calls out push/build/lint/typecheck success. A validator following only `validation-contract.md` could miss the README requirement.

rejectedObservations.observation: The push/build review repeated the same frontend skill issue about pre-existing failing regression tests counting toward test-first repair work.
